Type.description: Specifies the type of the conductor, e.g. if it is rigid or stranded.

Rigid.description: Used for conductors that are made of solid material.

Stranded.description: Used for conductors that are made of multiple individual strands (used for most automotive cores).

Foil.description: Used for conductors that are a foil (e.g. some shields).

Braided.description: Used for conductors that are made of multiple individual strands that are braided together (often used for shields).

ReferencedWireEnd.description:
References the wire ends that are used for the wire mounting. The minimum cardinality is one, because a wire mounting without wire end makes no sense.

The maximum cardinality is * in order to support multi crimps.

WireMountingDetail.description: Specifies the WireMoutingDetails, if a detailed description of the relationships between WireEnds and WireReceptions is needed.

CoreCrimpSize.description: Defines the required core crimp size (width & height) of this specific wire mounting situation.

DigitalRepresentationIndex.description:
An arbitrary change index that indicates if the digital representation (the content in VEC) of thisDocumentVersionhas been changed / regenerated. This can be for example an index, a timestamp or a checksum. This allows the detection of changes in the content, even when the DocumentNumber & DocumentVersion is the same.

For a more detailed explanation in the context see 'Parts & Documents'. KBLFRM-837.

PositionOnWire.description:
Specifies the order of theWireEndson theWireElementReference. This must be a value between 0 and 1. ThePositionOnWireis defined as a floating point number, to allow the ordering of an arbitrary number ofWireEndswhile having constant known values for the first and lastWireEnd('0.0' & '1.0'). ThePositionOnWireisnotdefined as a relative factor with respect to theWireLengthto determine an exact position of theWireEndon the wire. This would not be defined unambiguously, since aWireElementReferencecan define multiple lengths of differentWireLengthTypes. If such an interpretation can be made, it is tool or process specific definition.

In case ofWireRolewith multipleWireElementReferencesthe ordering of theWireEndsshall be consistent for allWireElementReferences.That means that allWireEndswithPositionOnWire = 0.0are on the same side of theWireRole.

StrippingLength.description: Defines the length by which an insulation on this stripped relative to thisWireEnd,defined by thecutBackLength. E.g. acutBackLengthof 20mm and astrippingLengthof 10mm means, that thisWireElementReferenceis cutback by 20mm relative to length of whole wire, on the side of the definingWireEnd.Form the remainingWireElementReferenceare then 10mm insulation removed.

PositionOnWire.description:
Specifies the order of theWireEndson theWireElementReference. This must be a value between 0 and 1. ThePositionOnWireis defined as a floating point number, to allow the ordering of an arbitrary number ofWireEndswhile having constant known values for the first and lastWireEnd('0.0' & '1.0'). ThePositionOnWireisnotdefined as a relative factor with respect to theWireLengthto determine an exact position of theWireEndon the wire. This would not be defined unambiguously, since aWireElementReferencecan define multiple lengths of differentWireLengthTypes. If such an interpretation can be made, it is tool or process specific definition.

In case ofWireRolewith multipleWireElementReferencesthe ordering of theWireEndsshall be consistent for allWireElementReferences.That means that allWireEndswithPositionOnWire = 0.0are on the same side of theWireRole.

StrippingLength.description: Defines the length by which an insulation on this stripped relative to thisWireEnd,defined by thecutBackLength. E.g. acutBackLengthof 20mm and astrippingLengthof 10mm means, that thisWireElementReferenceis cutback by 20mm relative to length of whole wire, on the side of the definingWireEnd.Form the remainingWireElementReferenceare then 10mm insulation removed.

For all VEC-documents a Specification-instance can be trusted to be identical if the DocumentVersion-instance is the same (see DocumentVersion) and the identification of the Specification is the same.

BaseColor.title: ARGB32ColorType
BaseColor.description: Specifies the base color of the insulation.

FirstIdentificationColor.description: Specifies the first identification color of the insulation.

SecondIdentificationColor.description: Specifies the second identification color of the insulation.

UnitComponent.title: EUInformation
UnitComponent.description: References the unit of the value.

ValueComponent.description: Specifies the value of the numerical value.

LowerBoundary.description: Specifies the lower boundary for the tolerance.

UpperBoundary.description: Specifies the upper boundary for the tolerance.

SubWireElement.description: Defines thesubWireElementsof thisWireElement. ThesubWireElementsshall be consistent with thesubWireElementSpecificationsof theWireElementSpecificationreferenced by thisWireElementand shall resemble that hierarchy.

items.description: Stand-in for the infinite remainder of this data structure, which is recursively defined.
